name: Finger-lickin' Chicken with Cheesy Dip
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken breasts into small pieces and place in a food processor or mincer. Process until finely minced. Transfer to a large bowl.
2. Add the thyme, cheese, and 50 g of breadcrumbs. Mix well, seasoning lightly with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
3. Using floured hands, shape the mixture into about 20 walnut-sized nuggets.
4. Pour the beaten egg onto a plate and sprinkle the remaining breadcrumbs onto another plate. Dip each nugget in the egg, then coat with breadcrumbs.
5. Heat the oil in a deep fryer or deep pan until it reaches 170°C/325°F or until a cube of bread browns in 30-40 seconds when dropped into the hot oil. Fry the nuggets in three batches for 5 minutes each. Drain on kitchen paper. To ensure the chicken is thoroughly cooked, cut a nugget in half after frying and check that the meat is no longer pink in the middle.
6. Mix all the ingredients together to make the cheesy dip until smooth. Garnish the nuggets with mustard and cress, and serve with the cheesy dip in a separate bowl garnished with chives.
Cals per serving: 647
Budget Tip: For a cheaper alternative to chicken breasts, try turkey mince.
